findings.message: loadItemsQuietly() catches fetchItems() failures and returns an empty array with no rethrow and no telemetry. The previous code at least set an error state (rendering a role=alert); the new code swallows the failure entirely. The error never reaches the window 'error' handler or the js-errors RUM counter, so a fully broken Items view (backend down, network error) renders as a normal-looking empty list and is completely invisible to monitoring. The empty list is indistinguishable from a legitimately empty result.
findings.suggestion: Do not swallow the error. Either rethrow after recording it, or explicitly increment the js-errors RUM counter / emit a beacon to POST /internal/rum before returning a fallback, and restore the user-visible error state (setError(true)) so the failure is both observable and surfaced. At minimum call the RUM error-reporting path (or rethrow so the window 'error'/unhandledrejection handler fires) in the catch block.
